perturbation.original_text: The term “ Information ” means (i) all financial, technical and other non-public or proprietary information which is furnished or disclosed orally, in writing, electronically or in other form or media by Disclosing Party and/or its Representatives to Recipient and/or its Representatives in connection with the Purpose and that is described or identified (at the time of disclosure) as being non-public, confidential or proprietary, or the non-public or proprietary nature of which is apparent from the context of the disclosure or the contents or nature of the information disclosed; (ii) all memoranda, notes, reports, files, copies, extracts, inventions, discoveries, improvements or any other thing prepared or derived from the information described in §1(a)(i), above; (iii) all CEII (as such term is defined below and only if CEII is exchanged under this Non-Disclosure Agreement); (iv) all Personal Information (as defined in the ISA Exhibit and only if Personal Information is exchanged under this Non-Disclosure Agreement); and (v) all Customer Information (as such term in is defined below and only if Customer Information is exchanged under this Non-Disclosure Agreement).
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Information ” means only the financial reports, which is furnished or disclosed orally, in writing, electronically or in other form or media by Disclosing Party and/or its Representatives to Recipient and/or its Representatives in connection with the Purpose and that is described or identified (at the time of disclosure) as being non-public, confidential or proprietary, or the non-public or proprietary nature of which is apparent from the context of the disclosure or the contents or nature of the information disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: This change restricts the definition of 'Information' to only include financial reports, but at the same time, the following sections §1(a)(iii), §1(a)(iv) and §1(a)(v) include CEII, Personal Information and Customer Information. Thus it creates an in-text contradiction by delimiting the definition and then using in the other clauses those elements that are not included in the defined Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) The term “ Information ” means (i) all financial, technical and other non-public or proprietary information which is furnished or disclosed orally, in writing, electronically or in other form or media by Disclosing Party and/or its Representatives to Recipient and/or its Representatives in connection with the Purpose and that is described or identified (at the time of disclosure) as being non-public, confidential or proprietary, or the non-public or proprietary nature of which is apparent from the context of the disclosure or the contents or nature of the information disclosed; (ii) all memoranda, notes, reports, files, copies, extracts, inventions, discoveries, improvements or any other thing prepared or derived from the information described in §1(a)(i), above; (iii) all CEII (as such term is defined below and only if CEII is exchanged under this Non-Disclosure Agreement); (iv) all Personal Information (as defined in the ISA Exhibit and only if Personal Information is exchanged under this Non-Disclosure Agreement); and (v) all Customer Information (as such term in is defined below and only if Customer Information is exchanged under this Non-Disclosure Agreement).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Information' to include only 'financial reports'. The contradicted text defines 'Information' to include 'CEII, Personal Information and Customer Information'. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text limits the scope of 'Information', conflicting with the contradicted text that includes a broader range of information types under the same term. The explanation notes this issue as well, delimiting the definition and then using elements that are not included in the defined Information.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall receive all Information in strict confidence, shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Information, and, except to the extent expressly permitted by this Non-Disclosure Agreement, shall not divulge Information to any third party without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party. The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may disclose Information to its Representatives to the extent each such Representative has a need to know such Information for the Purpose contemplated by this Non-Disclosure Agreement and agrees to observe and comply with the obligations of Recipient under this Non- Disclosure Agreement with regard to such Information. Recipient shall immediately notify Disclosing Party regarding, and shall be responsible hereunder for, any breach of the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement to the extent caused by its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall receive all Information in strict confidence, shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Information, and, Recipient may divulge Information to any third party without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party. The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may disclose Information to its Representatives to the extent each such Representative has a need to know such Information for the Purpose contemplated by this Non-Disclosure Agreement and agrees to observe and comply with the obligations of Recipient under this Non- Disclosure Agreement with regard to such Information. Recipient shall immediately notify Disclosing Party regarding, and shall be responsible hereunder for, any breach of the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement to the extent caused by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'except to the extent expressly permitted by this Non-Disclosure Agreement, shall not' creates an in-text contradiction by allowing the recipient to divulge information to third parties without consent in the first sentence, while the next sentence talks about disclosure to representatives that agree to be bound by the non-disclosure, therefore implying that divulgation to third parties is not permitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall receive all Information in strict confidence, shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Information, and, except to the extent expressly permitted by this Non-Disclosure Agreement, shall not divulge Information to any third party without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party. The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may disclose Information to its Representatives to the extent each such Representative has a need to know such Information for the Purpose contemplated by this Non-Disclosure Agreement and agrees to observe and comply with the obligations of Recipient under this Non- Disclosure Agreement with regard to such Information. Recipient shall immediately notify Disclosing Party regarding, and shall be responsible hereunder for, any breach of the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement to the extent caused by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the restriction on divulging information to third parties without consent, directly contradicting the original intent and the contradicted text, which explicitly prohibits such disclosure except as permitted. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text now allows for unrestricted disclosure, while the contradicted text aims to prevent it, leading to an inconsistent and unenforceable agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not apply to Information that, (i) at the time of disclosure by or on behalf of Disclosing Party hereunder, is in the public domain, or thereafter enters the public domain without any breach of this Non- Disclosure Agreement by Recipient or any of its Representatives, (ii) is rightfully in the possession or knowledge of Recipient or its Representatives prior to its disclosure by or on behalf of Disclosing Party hereunder, (iii) is rightfully acquired by Recipient or its Representative(s) from a third party who is not under any obligation of confidence with respect to such Information, or (iv) is developed by Recipient or its Representatives independently of the Information disclosed hereunder by or on behalf of Disclosing Party (as evidenced by written documentation).
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not apply to Information that, (i) at the time of disclosure by or on behalf of Disclosing Party hereunder, is in the public domain, or thereafter enters the public domain without any breach of this Non- Disclosure Agreement by Recipient or any of its Representatives, (ii) is rightfully in the possession or knowledge of Recipient or its Representatives prior to its disclosure by or on behalf of Disclosing Party hereunder, (iii) is rightfully acquired by Recipient or its Representative(s) from a third party who is not under any obligation of confidence with respect to such Information, or (iv) is developed by Recipient or its Representatives independently of the Information disclosed hereunder by or on behalf of Disclosing Party (as evidenced by written documentation). This Non-Disclosure Agreement applies to all Information.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'This Non-Disclosure Agreement applies to all Information' we create a contradiction with the entire paragraph, since all of the prior clauses dictate scenarios that are excluded, and now it is including all of them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not apply to Information that, (i) at the time of disclosure by or on behalf of Disclosing Party hereunder, is in the public domain, or thereafter enters the public domain without any breach of this Non- Disclosure Agreement by Recipient or any of its Representatives, (ii) is rightfully in the possession or knowledge of Recipient or its Representatives prior to its disclosure by or on behalf of Disclosing Party hereunder, (iii) is rightfully acquired by Recipient or its Representative(s) from a third party who is not under any obligation of confidence with respect to such Information, or (iv) is developed by Recipient or its Representatives independently of the Information disclosed hereunder by or on behalf of Disclosing Party (as evidenced by written documentation).
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text define specific scenarios where the Non-Disclosure Agreement does *not* apply. By adding the sentence 'This Non-Disclosure Agreement applies to all Information' to the changed text, it creates a direct conflict, negating all the exceptions previously defined. The changed text asserts the NDA covers *all* information, including the information explicitly excluded in the original/contradicted text. This is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that if it or any of its Representatives are required by law, by a court or by other governmental or regulatory authorities (including, without limitation, by oral question, interrogatory, request for information or documents, subpoena, civil or criminal investigative demand or other process) to disclose any of Disclosing Party’s Information, Recipient shall provide Disclosing Party with prompt notice of any such request or requirement, to the extent permitted to do so by applicable law, so that Disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order or waive compliance with the provisions of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that if it or any of its Representatives are required by law, by a court or by other governmental or regulatory authorities (including, without limitation, by oral question, interrogatory, request for information or documents, subpoena, civil or criminal investigative demand or other process) to disclose any of Disclosing Party’s Information, Recipient is not obligated to provide Disclosing Party with prompt notice of any such request or requirement, to the extent permitted to do so by applicable law, so that Disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order or waive compliance with the provisions of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient shall provide Disclosing Party with prompt notice' to 'Recipient is not obligated to provide Disclosing Party with prompt notice' creates a direct contradiction in terms of notification requirements when disclosure is required by law, because it now does not have the responsability to notify the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that if it or any of its Representatives are required by law, by a court or by other governmental or regulatory authorities (including, without limitation, by oral question, interrogatory, request for information or documents, subpoena, civil or criminal investigative demand or other process) to disclose any of Disclosing Party’s Information, Recipient shall provide Disclosing Party with prompt notice of any such request or requirement, to the extent permitted to do so by applicable law, so that Disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order or waive compliance with the provisions of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Recipient shall provide Disclosing Party with prompt notice'. The changed text modifies this to 'Recipient is not obligated to provide Disclosing Party with prompt notice'. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction regarding the notification obligation. The contradicted text restates the original obligation to provide prompt notice, thereby highlighting the direct conflict introduced by the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall return and deliver, or cause to be returned and delivered, to Disclosing Party, or destroy or cause to be destroyed (with certification of destruction delivered to Disclosing Party), all tangible Information, including copies and abstracts thereof, within thirty (30) days of a written request by Disclosing Party (a “ Request ”). The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may retain one (1) copy of such Information for archival purposes only and subject to compliance with the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each Party agrees that Recipient shall not be required to return to Disclosing Party, or destroy, copies of Disclosing Party’s Information that (A) reside on Recipient’s or its Representatives’ backup, disaster recovery or business continuity systems, or (B) that Recipient or its Representatives are obligated by applicable law and/or governmental regulations to retain. Recipient agrees that, following its receipt of the Request, it shall neither retrieve nor use Disclosing Party’s Information for any purpose other than that specified in clause (B) above.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall return and deliver, or cause to be returned and delivered, to Disclosing Party, or destroy or cause to be destroyed (with certification of destruction delivered to Disclosing Party), all tangible Information, including copies and abstracts thereof, within thirty (30) days of a written request by Disclosing Party (a “ Request ”). The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may retain one (1) copy of such Information for archival purposes only and subject to compliance with the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each Party agrees that Recipient shall be required to return to Disclosing Party, or destroy, copies of Disclosing Party’s Information that (A) reside on Recipient’s or its Representatives’ backup, disaster recovery or business continuity systems, or (B) that Recipient or its Representatives are obligated by applicable law and/or governmental regulations to retain. Recipient agrees that, following its receipt of the Request, it shall neither retrieve nor use Disclosing Party’s Information for any purpose other than that specified in clause (B) above.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be required to return' to 'shall be required to return' creates a direct conflict. The original text allows for exceptions for archival purposes and legally required retention, while the altered text makes the return or destruction mandatory, even for those exceptions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall return and deliver, or cause to be returned and delivered, to Disclosing Party, or destroy or cause to be destroyed (with certification of destruction delivered to Disclosing Party), all tangible Information, including copies and abstracts thereof, within thirty (30) days of a written request by Disclosing Party (a “ Request ”). The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may retain one (1) copy of such Information for archival purposes only and subject to compliance with the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each Party agrees that Recipient shall not be required to return to Disclosing Party, or destroy, copies of Disclosing Party’s Information that (A) reside on Recipient’s or its Representatives’ backup, disaster recovery or business continuity systems, or (B) that Recipient or its Representatives are obligated by applicable law and/or governmental regulations to retain. Recipient agrees that, following its receipt of the Request, it shall neither retrieve nor use Disclosing Party’s Information for any purpose other than that specified in clause (B) above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'shall not be required to return' to 'shall be required to return', directly contradicting the earlier statement in the contradicted text that allows the Recipient to retain copies of Disclosing Party’s Information under specific circumstances (backup systems, legal obligations). This creates a clear conflict regarding the Recipient's obligations for returning or destroying the information, impacting enforceability and causing a strong in-text contradiction as the exceptions are removed, requiring return or destruction of all copies irrespective of the previously stipulated exceptions.

perturbation.original_text: Except with respect to any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure. In the case of any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall survive for (i) the Term, or (ii) so long as such Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, as applicable, is required to be kept confidential under applicable law, whichever period is longer (the “ Special Information Term ”). Either Party may terminate this Non-Disclosure Agreement by written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive (i) for the Special Information Term for all Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information disclosed prior to such termination, and (ii) for the Term for all other Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Except with respect to any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure. In the case of any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not survive for (i) the Term, or (ii) so long as such Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, as applicable, is required to be kept confidential under applicable law, whichever period is longer (the “ Special Information Term ”). Either Party may terminate this Non-Disclosure Agreement by written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive (i) for the Special Information Term for all Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information disclosed prior to such termination, and (ii) for the Term for all other Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall survive for' to 'shall not survive for' for Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information directly contradicts the final sentence which states 'Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive'. Now, Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information does not survive, but then later is stated that they do.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except with respect to any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure. In the case of any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall survive for (i) the Term, or (ii) so long as such Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, as applicable, is required to be kept confidential under applicable law, whichever period is longer (the “ Special Information Term ”). Either Party may terminate this Non-Disclosure Agreement by written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive (i) for the Special Information Term for all Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information disclosed prior to such termination, and (ii) for the Term for all other Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies a crucial clause regarding the survival of obligations related to Customer Information, CEII, or Personal Information after termination. Originally, these obligations 'shall survive for' a specified period. The change replaces this with 'shall not survive for', directly contradicting the later sentence: 'Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive (i) for the Special Information Term for all Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information disclosed prior to such termination.' This creates a clear in-text contradiction as the modified text states that these obligations do not survive termination, while the contradicted text states they do. This is a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.
